member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Backfill.BackfillRequest
member.summary: Incoming request describing a historical backfill.
member.param: The name of the historical data provider to use.
Maximum number of symbols to process concurrently. Overrides the value from when set.
Optional per-symbol priority map; lower value means processed first. Keys are matched case-insensitively. Symbols not listed are treated as priority 0.

When true and a is provided to , symbols that were successfully checkpointed in a previous run are skipped. Symbols with a checkpoint have their date advanced to the day after the last recorded checkpoint date, reducing redundant work on restart.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Backfill.BackfillStatusStore
member.summary: Persists and reads last backfill status so both the collector and UI can surface progress. Also tracks per-symbol checkpoints so interrupted jobs can be resumed without re-fetching bars that were already written successfully.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Backfill.BackfillStatusStore.WriteSymbolCheckpointAsync(System.String,System.DateOnly,System.Int64,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Records that was successfully backfilled up to and including with bars. Subsequent calls update the stored date if the new value is later than the previously recorded one; the bar count is replaced unconditionally when the date advances. Thread-safe: concurrent writes are serialized through an internal lock.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Backfill.BackfillStatusStore.TryReadSymbolCheckpoints
member.summary: Returns the per-symbol checkpoint map, or null when no checkpoints have been saved yet. Keys are symbol names (case-insensitive); values are the last successfully completed date.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Backfill.BackfillStatusStore.TryReadSymbolBarCounts
member.summary: Returns the per-symbol bar-count map persisted alongside the checkpoints, or null when no bar-count data has been saved yet. Keys are symbol names (case-insensitive); values are the number of bars written in the run that last advanced the checkpoint date for that symbol.

member.summary: Removes all per-symbol checkpoints and bar-count data. Call when starting a fresh (non-resume) backfill run.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Backfill.SymbolValidationSignal
member.summary: Per-symbol completeness signal produced at the end of a backfill run.
member.param: The ticker symbol this signal relates to.

"Pass" — symbol fetched (or confirmed already covered) with at least one bar. "Warn" — symbol fetched successfully but the provider returned zero bars. "Fail" — symbol could not be fetched due to an error.
Bars written during this run. Zero for symbols that were skipped because their checkpoint already covered the requested range.
Bar count stored in the checkpoint from the previous run. Non-null only when is "Pass" and the symbol was skipped (resume mode, fully covered). Allows callers to reconcile expected vs. actual counts.
Inclusive start date actually fetched this run (post-resume-advance).
Inclusive end date actually fetched this run.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Canonicalization.CanonicalizingPublisher
member.summary: Decorator that canonicalizes events before forwarding them to the inner publisher. Wraps any to apply symbol resolution, venue normalization, and condition code mapping transparently.

remarks.para: Phase 2 (Dual-Write): When DualWriteEnabled is true, the raw event is published first, then the canonicalized version. This allows downstream consumers to validate parity between raw and enriched paths.
Phase 3 (Canonical-only): When dual-write is off, only the canonicalized event is published.
Pilot symbol filtering limits canonicalization to a configurable subset during rollout. Events for non-pilot symbols pass through unchanged.

Quarantine Sink: When a is provided via the quarantine constructor parameter, events whose symbol cannot be resolved are written to the quarantine file in addition to being published with raw data. This prevents unmappable events from silently accumulating with no audit trail.

member.summary: Total events successfully canonicalized.

member.summary: Events with unresolved canonical symbols.

member.summary: Events dual-written (raw + canonical).

member.summary: Events with unresolved symbols sent to the quarantine sink.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Composition.CircuitBreakerCallbackRouter
member.summary: Static bridge that receives Polly circuit breaker state-change notifications and forwards them to the singleton.
member.remarks: This indirection is required because Polly callback delegates are captured as closures during DI service registration (before the container is built), while is only resolvable after the container is built. is called once by after the root service provider is constructed.

member.summary: Registers core ledger services into the DI container.

item.description: — a singleton keyed ledger store that manages independent in-memory instances per strategy run or project. Consumers resolve it to obtain or create a by without having to manage ledger lifetime themselves.
LedgerReadService — lives in Meridian.Strategies, which is not referenced by Meridian.Application. It is registered by UI host startup code.
itself — created per-run by the backtesting engine and strategy execution layer; it is a domain object, not an injectable singleton.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Composition.Features.ProviderFeatureRegistration.RegisterOptionsChainProviders(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection)
member.summary:
Registers implementations in priority order.
If Alpaca credentials are present, Alpaca is selected as the single active provider.
Else if Polygon credentials are present, Polygon is selected.
Otherwise, the is used as the fallback.
resolves IOptionsChainProvider via GetService<IOptionsChainProvider>(); Microsoft DI returns the last registration for single-service resolution. All providers are also exposed via IEnumerable<IOptionsChainProvider> for enumeration and health checks.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Composition.Startup.ModeRunners.CollectorModeRunner
member.summary: Runs the headless streaming data collector. Handles provider creation (with optional failover), symbol subscriptions, hot-reload, and simulate-feed. Does not start a UI server — desktop mode uses which wraps this runner.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Composition.Startup.ModeRunners.CollectorModeRunner.RunAsync(Meridian.Application.Composition.Startup.StartupModels.StartupContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Executes the streaming collector loop. Creates and configures the host, connects to the data provider, applies symbol subscriptions, and runs until the process is cancelled or the provider disconnects.
member.returns: Exit code: 0 on clean shutdown, non-zero on error.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Composition.Startup.ModeRunners.CommandModeRunner
member.summary: Handles one-shot CLI command dispatch (help, validate-config, symbols, dry-run, etc.). Attempts to dispatch the raw args through the command dispatcher. Returns the exit code when a command was handled, or null to signal pass-through to the next startup phase.

member.summary: Tries to dispatch a CLI command from the startup context.
member.returns: The command's exit code when a command was matched and handled; null when no command matched and startup should continue to a runtime mode.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Composition.Startup.ModeRunners.DesktopModeRunner
member.summary: Runs the desktop host mode: starts the embedded HTTP UI server, then executes either the backfill or streaming collector depending on the request, and finally shuts the server down. Desktop mode routing (backfill vs. collector) is resolved here since the orchestrator uses a single plan entry for all desktop invocations.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Composition.Startup.ModeRunners.DesktopModeRunner.RunAsync(Meridian.Application.Composition.Startup.StartupModels.StartupContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Starts the desktop UI server, runs the appropriate data operation (backfill or streaming), then stops and disposes the server on completion.
member.returns: Exit code from the inner runner.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Composition.Startup.ModeRunners.WebModeRunner
member.summary: Runs the standalone web dashboard server. Starts the HTTP server, waits for Ctrl+C or cancellation, then shuts it down gracefully. No data collector is started in this mode.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Composition.Startup.ModeRunners.WebModeRunner.RunAsync(Meridian.Application.Composition.Startup.StartupModels.StartupContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Starts the web dashboard and blocks until the process is cancelled or the user presses Ctrl+C.

member.summary: Sequences the named startup phases and delegates execution to the appropriate mode runner.

item.description: Command dispatch — one-shot CLI commands exit here.
Web mode check — HTTP-only dashboard exits here (skips validation).
Validation — configuration, file permissions, schema compatibility.
Runtime selection — desktop, backfill, or streaming collector.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Config.ConfigurationPipeline.ApplySelfHealingFixes(Meridian.Application.Config.AppConfig,Meridian.Application.Config.SelfHealingStrictness)
member.summary: Discovers and conditionally applies self-healing fixes to based on the requested level.
member.returns:
A tuple of:
Config — the (potentially modified) configuration.
Applied — fixes that were applied to the config.
Refused — -level fixes that were not applied because is .
Warnings — advisory messages that are not actionable fixes.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Config.PipelineOptions
member.summary: Options for controlling the configuration pipeline behavior.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Config.PipelineOptions.ApplySelfHealing
member.summary: Whether to apply self-healing fixes to the configuration.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Config.PipelineOptions.ValidateConfig
member.summary: Whether to validate the configuration.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Config.PipelineOptions.HealingStrictness
member.summary: Controls how aggressively self-healing behaviours are applied. (default) applies all fixes and logs significant ones as warnings. applies only cosmetic changes and refuses -level fixes with a startup error so that operators must correct the configuration manually.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Config.PipelineOptions.Default
member.summary: Default options — self-healing and validation enabled, strictness from MDC_CONFIG_STRICTNESS environment variable (defaults to Development).

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Config.PipelineOptions.Strict
member.summary: Options for strict validation without self-healing.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Config.PipelineOptions.Lenient
member.summary: Options for lenient loading (no validation, no self-healing).

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Config.PipelineOptions.ParseStrictness(System.String)
member.summary: Parses the MDC_CONFIG_STRICTNESS environment variable (or any string value) into a level. Recognised values: production, prod, strict (case-insensitive). Everything else — including null — resolves to .

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Config.SelfHealingStrictness
member.summary: Controls how self-healing reacts to significant configuration issues.

member.name: F:Meridian.Application.Config.SelfHealingStrictness.Development
member.summary: All self-healing fixes are applied. -level changes are applied but logged as Warning. Suitable for local development and CI environments where operator attention is not required for every startup.

member.name: F:Meridian.Application.Config.SelfHealingStrictness.Production
member.summary: Only safe cosmetic fixes () are applied silently. -level fixes are refused and surfaced as validation errors so that the process cannot start until a human corrects the configuration. Use MDC_CONFIG_STRICTNESS=production to enable.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Config.SelfHealingSeverity
member.summary: Indicates how impactful a self-healing fix is, which determines how it is handled under different levels.

member.name: F:Meridian.Application.Config.SelfHealingSeverity.AutoFix
member.summary: Safe, cosmetic correction (e.g., clamping an out-of-range value, normalising a format string, resolving credentials from environment variables). Applied silently in all environments.

member.name: F:Meridian.Application.Config.SelfHealingSeverity.Warn
member.summary: Significant behavioural change (e.g., switching the active data provider, adding a default symbol set). Applied with a Warning-level log entry in ; refused with a startup error in .

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Config.SelfHealingFix
member.summary: Describes a single self-healing change that was proposed by .

member.summary: Describes a single self-healing change that was proposed by .

member.summary: Human-readable description of the change.

member.summary: How impactful the change is.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Config.DeploymentContext
member.summary: Unified deployment context that captures all deployment-related settings at startup. This reduces conditional branching across entry points by centralizing deployment mode logic.

item.description: Headless - Console application, no UI, suitable for servers/containers
Web - HTTP server with web dashboard on configurable port
Desktop - Native desktop app with embedded HTTP server for local UI

remarks.para: The context is immutable once created and provides all deployment-related decisions through computed properties, eliminating scattered conditional logic.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Config.SensitiveValueMasker.PasswordRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
(Password|Pwd)\\s*=\\s*[^;]+;
Options:
RegexOptions.IgnoreCase
Explanation:
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match a character in the set [Pp].
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match an empty string.
○ Match a character in the set [Ss] exactly 2 times.
○ Match a character in the set [Ww].
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match a character in the set [Dd].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match an empty string.
○ Match a character in the set [Dd].
○ Match a whitespace character atomically any number of times.
○ Match '='.
○ Match a whitespace character greedily any number of times.
○ Match a character other than ';' atomically at least once.
○ Match ';'.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Config.SensitiveValueMasker.SecretRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
(Secret)\\s*=\\s*[^;]+;
Options:
RegexOptions.IgnoreCase
Explanation:
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match a character in the set [Ss].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Cc].
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Tt].
○ Match a whitespace character atomically any number of times.
○ Match '='.
○ Match a whitespace character greedily any number of times.
○ Match a character other than ';' atomically at least once.
○ Match ';'.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Config.SensitiveValueMasker.AccountKeyRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
(AccountKey)\\s*=\\s*[^;]+;
Options:
RegexOptions.IgnoreCase
Explanation:
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match a character in the set [Aa].
○ Match a character in the set [Cc] exactly 2 times.
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match a character in the set [Uu].
○ Match a character in the set [Nn].
○ Match a character in the set [Tt].
○ Match a character in the set [Kk\u212A].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Yy].
○ Match a whitespace character atomically any number of times.
○ Match '='.
○ Match a whitespace character greedily any number of times.
○ Match a character other than ';' atomically at least once.
○ Match ';'.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Coordination.ClusterCoordinatorService
member.summary: Background service that maintains a single cluster coordinator via shared-storage lease competition. Acquires the leader/cluster-coordinator lease on startup and renews it while running. When the lease cannot be acquired or renewed, leadership is relinquished and the instance transitions to follower mode.
member.remarks: Registered as both (singleton) and a hosted service so that the election loop starts automatically with the application lifetime.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Coordination.ClusterCoordinatorService.ExecuteAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Runs the election loop: attempts to become leader every 5 seconds while coordination is enabled.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Coordination.SplitBrainDetector
member.summary: Background service that detects and heals split-brain conditions in the Meridian cluster. A split-brain occurs when two instances simultaneously believe they hold the coordinator lease — typically caused by a transient network partition or storage hiccup.

remarks.para: Every 5 seconds this service reads all active coordinator leases from the shared store. If exactly one (or zero) coordinator lease is found, the cluster is healthy and a secondary check scans for symbols subscribed by multiple instances simultaneously.

When two or more coordinator leases are detected, the instance with the lexicographically lower yields its lease by calling . This deterministic tiebreak guarantees convergence without requiring any additional external coordination.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Coordination.SplitBrainDetector.ExecuteAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Runs the split-brain detection loop until the application stops.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Credentials.ICredentialStore
member.summary: Centralized credential store providing unified access to API credentials across all providers with caching, refresh, and validation capabilities.
member.remarks:
The credential store addresses scattered credential management by providing: - Single interface for all credential types (API keys, OAuth, etc.) - Automatic loading from environment variables with fallback - Caching with configurable TTL - Token refresh for OAuth providers - Validation and health checking - Metadata for discovery and documentation
Replaces scattered patterns: - Direct Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable calls - CredentialProvider inconsistent implementations - OAuthTokenRefreshService OAuth-only handling

member.summary: Enables automatic calibration of expected events per hour based on the observed event rate after the initial calibration window has elapsed. When enabled and no explicit liquidity profile has been registered for a symbol, the calculator will adjust its expectation to match the symbol's observed activity, preventing grossly incorrect scores for symbols whose actual rate differs from .

member.summary: Minimum number of events that must have been observed within the calibration window before auto-calibration is applied. Prevents calibration on symbols with very sparse, possibly anomalous early-day activity.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Monitoring.DataQuality.IQualityAnalyzer`1
member.summary: Base interface for quality analyzers providing plugin-style architecture. Analyzers can be discovered, registered, and run by the quality analysis engine.
member.typeparam: Type of data the analyzer processes.
member.remarks:
Addresses orphaned analyzer implementations by providing: - Standardized interface for all quality analyzers - Discovery and registration mechanism - Configurable analysis parameters - Consistent result format
Existing analyzers to migrate: - LatencyHistogramAnalyzer - CompletenessScoreCalculator - GapAnalyzer - AnomalyDetector - SequenceErrorTracker

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Monitoring.BadTickFilter
member.summary: Filters and detects bad ticks (invalid market data points) using multiple heuristics to identify data quality issues. Part of the data quality framework (DQ-20).
member.remarks: Bad ticks can include: - Prices far outside normal trading range - Zero or negative volumes on trades - Impossible price movements within a time window - Prices at obvious placeholder values (0.01, 9999.99, etc.)

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Monitoring.LuldBand
member.summary: Represents a LULD (Limit Up/Limit Down) price band for a symbol. Under SEC Rule 201 / Reg NMS Plan, trades outside these bands should be rejected. Bands are recalculated every 5 minutes during regular trading hours using the average reference price of the prior 5-minute period.
member.remarks: Price band percentages vary by tier: - Tier 1 (S&P 500, Russell 1000, select ETPs): 5% for prices >$3, lesser of $0.15 or 75% for prices <=$3 - Tier 2 (other NMS stocks): 10% for prices >$3, lesser of $0.15 or 75% for prices <=$3 - During first/last 15 minutes of trading: bands are doubled

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Monitoring.LuldBand.#ctor(System.Decimal,System.Decimal,System.Decimal,System.DateTimeOffset)
member.summary: Represents a LULD (Limit Up/Limit Down) price band for a symbol. Under SEC Rule 201 / Reg NMS Plan, trades outside these bands should be rejected. Bands are recalculated every 5 minutes during regular trading hours using the average reference price of the prior 5-minute period.
member.remarks: Price band percentages vary by tier: - Tier 1 (S&P 500, Russell 1000, select ETPs): 5% for prices >$3, lesser of $0.15 or 75% for prices <=$3 - Tier 2 (other NMS stocks): 10% for prices >$3, lesser of $0.15 or 75% for prices <=$3 - During first/last 15 minutes of trading: bands are doubled

The new state being transitioned to.

Optional number of failures that triggered the transition.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Monitoring.PrometheusMetrics
member.summary:
Prometheus metrics exporter for Meridian. Implements best practices from prometheus-net documentation and production patterns from Grafana dashboard templates.
Naming conventions follow Prometheus best practices: - snake_case for metric names - _total suffix for counters - Descriptive help text - Appropriate metric types (Counter, Gauge, Histogram)
Cardinality guards: Symbol-labeled metrics are gated by a configurable allowlist. Symbols not in the allowlist are aggregated under "__other__". This prevents metric explosion with dynamic/large symbol universes.

member.name: F:Meridian.Application.Monitoring.PrometheusMetrics._maxSymbolLabels
member.summary: Maximum number of symbols allowed as metric labels before aggregation kicks in.

member.name: F:Meridian.Application.Monitoring.PrometheusMetrics._admittedSymbols
member.summary: Tracks which symbols have been admitted to labeled metrics. Once the cap is reached, new symbols go to "__other__".

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Monitoring.PrometheusMetrics.SetMaxSymbolLabels(System.Int32)
member.summary: Configures the maximum number of distinct symbol labels allowed in metrics. Symbols beyond this limit are aggregated under "__other__".

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Monitoring.PrometheusMetrics.AdmitSymbol(System.String)
member.summary: Pre-admits specific symbols to the metrics label set. Call during startup for known monitored symbols.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Monitoring.PrometheusMetrics.GetSymbolLabel(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the symbol label to use for metrics. If the symbol is admitted, returns it directly. Otherwise returns "__other__" to prevent cardinality explosion.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Monitoring.PrometheusMetrics.UpdateFromSnapshot
member.summary: Updates all Prometheus metrics from the current Metrics snapshot. Should be called periodically (e.g., every 1-5 seconds) to keep metrics current.

member.summary: Updates Prometheus validation metrics from a .

member.summary: Records a trade event with symbol and venue labels. Uses cardinality guard to prevent label explosion with many symbols.

member.summary: Records event processing latency in microseconds.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Monitoring.StatusHttpServer
member.summary: Lightweight HTTP server exposing runtime status, metrics (Prometheus format), and a minimal HTML dashboard. Avoids pulling in ASP.NET for small deployments. Uses shared StatusEndpointHandlers for consistent response generation with UiServer. Enhanced with detailed health check (QW-32), backpressure status (MON-18), and provider latency (PROV-11).

Registers extended providers for detailed health, backpressure, and provider latency endpoints.

member.summary: Validates that prices conform to expected tick size increments. Part of the data quality framework (DQ-13) to detect prices that don't match the exchange's minimum price increment rules.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.DeadLetterSink
member.summary: Persists events that failed validation to a JSONL dead-letter file for later inspection and replay. Each line contains the original event together with the validation errors that caused rejection.
member.remarks: The dead-letter directory is created lazily on the first rejected event to avoid unnecessary file-system operations when all events pass validation. A serialises writes so the file remains well-formed even when multiple callers record rejections concurrently.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.DualPathEventPipeline
member.summary: A dual-path event pipeline that routes high-volume and events through a zero-allocation hot path (struct ring buffer), while routing all other event types through the standard record-based .

remarks.para: Hot path (Trade / BboQuote): The producer extracts the essential fields into a or struct and writes it into a pre-allocated . No heap allocation occurs on the producer thread. A dedicated background consumer drains the ring buffer in batches, reconstructs objects, and forwards them to the slow path for storage.
Slow path (all other event types): Events are passed directly to the underlying , preserving all existing WAL, deduplication, and validation behaviour.
Zero-allocation producer API: Callers that already construct structs without a intermediary can call or directly. These overloads bypass allocation entirely.
Ring buffer full behaviour: When a ring buffer is full the event falls back to the slow path to prevent data loss.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.DualPathEventPipeline.#ctor(Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipeline,Meridian.Core.Performance.SymbolTable,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Boolean,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{Meridian.Application.Pipeline.DualPathEventPipeline})
member.summary: Creates a that wraps an existing slow path.
member.param: The underlying used for non-hot-path event types and as a fallback when ring buffers are full. The caller retains ownership and must dispose it separately after this pipeline.
Shared symbol intern table. Must be the same instance used by any callers that invoke / directly.
Capacity of each ring buffer (one for trades, one for quotes). Rounded up to the next power of two. Default is 4 096 slots.
Maximum events drained per consumer iteration. Default is 256.
When the background consumer tasks are not started. Intended for unit tests that need to inspect ring buffer state without a concurrent consumer draining it. Defaults to .

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.DualPathEventPipeline.TryPublish(Meridian.Domain.Events.MarketEvent@)
member.summary: Routes the event to the hot path (ring buffer) for trades and quotes, or to the slow path for all other event types.
member.remarks: When the trade or quote ring buffer is full the event is forwarded to the slow-path to prevent data loss.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.DualPathEventPipeline.TryPublishTrade(Meridian.Core.Performance.RawTradeEvent@)
member.summary: Writes a struct directly into the trade ring buffer without any heap allocation on the calling (producer) thread.
member.returns: when the event was written; when the ring buffer was full and the event was dropped.

member.summary: Writes a struct directly into the quote ring buffer without any heap allocation on the calling (producer) thread.
member.returns: when the event was written; when the ring buffer was full and the event was dropped.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipeline
member.summary: High-throughput, backpressured pipeline that decouples producers from storage sinks. Includes periodic flushing, capacity monitoring, performance metrics, and optional Write-Ahead Log (WAL) integration for crash-safe durability.
member.remarks: When a is provided, the pipeline ensures events are persisted to the WAL before being written to the primary storage sink. On startup, replays any uncommitted WAL records to the sink, preventing data loss from crashes. The consumer writes each event to the WAL, then to the sink, and commits the WAL after each batch is flushed. Both and defer WAL writes to the consumer to ensure each event is recorded exactly once, preventing duplicate records during recovery.

member.name: F:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipeline.DefaultFinalFlushTimeout
member.summary: Default maximum time to wait for the final flush during shutdown before giving up. Prevents the consumer task from hanging indefinitely if the sink is unresponsive.

member.name: F:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipeline.DefaultSinkFlushTimeout
member.summary: Default per-call sink flush timeout for periodic flushes. Prevents a hung sink from stalling the pipeline indefinitely.
Creates a new EventPipeline with configurable capacity and flush behavior.
member.param: The storage sink for persisting events.
Maximum number of events the queue can hold. Default is 100,000.
Behavior when the queue is full. Default is DropOldest.
Interval between periodic flushes. Default is 5 seconds.
Number of events to batch before writing. Default is 100.
Whether to enable periodic flushing. Default is true.
Optional logger for error reporting. When provided, enables logging for flush failures and disposal errors.
Optional audit trail for tracking dropped events.
Optional Write-Ahead Log for crash-safe durability. When provided, events are written to the WAL before the primary sink. Call on startup to replay any uncommitted records from a prior crash.
Optional event metrics for tracking pipeline throughput.
Optional timeout for the final flush during shutdown. Defaults to 30 seconds.
Optional per-call timeout for periodic sink flushes. Prevents a hung sink from stalling the pipeline indefinitely. Defaults to 60 seconds.
Optional event validator for pre-persistence validation.
Optional dead-letter sink for rejected events.
Optional persistent deduplication ledger for suppressing duplicate events.
Requested number of consumer tasks for the slow path. Values greater than 1 are honored only when WAL, validation, dead-letter routing, and deduplication are disabled.

member.summary: Creates a new EventPipeline with a shared policy for capacity and backpressure.
member.param: The storage sink for persisting events.
The pipeline policy controlling channel capacity and backpressure.
Interval between periodic flushes. Default is 5 seconds.
Number of events to batch before writing. Default is 100.
Whether to enable periodic flushing. Default is true.
Optional audit trail for tracking dropped events.
Optional Write-Ahead Log for crash-safe durability.
Optional event metrics for tracking pipeline throughput.
Optional timeout for the final flush during shutdown. Defaults to 30 seconds.
Optional per-call timeout for periodic sink flushes. Defaults to 60 seconds.
Optional event validator. When provided, events that fail validation are routed to the and excluded from primary storage.
Optional dead-letter sink for events rejected by the validator.
Optional persistent deduplication ledger for suppressing duplicate events.
Requested number of consumer tasks for the slow path. Values greater than 1 are honored only when WAL, validation, dead-letter routing, and deduplication are disabled.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipeline.RecoverAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Recovers uncommitted events from the WAL and replays them to the storage sink. Call this method once on startup, before publishing new events, to ensure data from a prior crash is not lost.
member.remarks: This method initializes the WAL and reads any records that were written but not committed (i.e., not yet confirmed persisted to the primary sink). Each recovered event is written to the sink and then the WAL is committed. If no WAL is configured, this method is a no-op.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipeline.TryPublish(Meridian.Domain.Events.MarketEvent@)
member.summary: Attempts to publish an event to the pipeline without blocking. Returns false if the queue is full (event will be dropped based on FullMode).
member.remarks: WAL writes are deferred to the consumer task to preserve the non-blocking contract of this method. Events enqueued via this path carry WalSequence == 0; the consumer detects this and appends the event to the WAL before forwarding it to the storage sink, maintaining the same durability guarantee as at the cost of a slightly larger crash-recovery window. Callers that require the event to be WAL-persisted before this call returns should use instead.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipeline.TryPublishWithResult(Meridian.Domain.Events.MarketEvent@)
member.summary: Attempts to publish an event and returns a that describes the outcome in detail — accepted, accepted under pressure, or dropped.
member.remarks: Use this method when the caller needs to react to backpressure (e.g. pause a subscription, log a drop, or adjust polling rate). For fire-and-forget callers the original remains the recommended hot-path method.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipeline.RecordDrop(Meridian.Domain.Events.MarketEvent@)
member.summary: Records a dropped event — shared by DropWrite pre-check and Wait-mode TryWrite failure.

member.name: F:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipeline.EventTypeNames
member.summary: Cached enum name lookup — avoids Enum.ToString() allocation per event.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipeline.PublishAsync(Meridian.Domain.Events.MarketEvent,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Publishes an event to the pipeline, waiting if necessary. When WAL is enabled, the event is appended to the WAL before the queue handoff so a successful publish is crash-safe even if the consumer has not run yet.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipeline.Complete
member.summary: Signals that no more events will be published.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipeline.FlushAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Waits for the consumer to process all currently-queued events, then forces an immediate flush of buffered data to storage.
member.remarks: If events were dropped due to backpressure during the flush window, a warning is logged. The flush still writes all events that were consumed to storage — it does not suppress the flush because of drops — but callers should treat the warning as an indication that the result set is incomplete.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.FSharpEventValidator
member.summary: Validates instances by delegating to the F# Railway-Oriented validation pipeline (Meridian.FSharp.Validation).

remarks.para: Trade and BBO-quote events are validated against the F# validators. All other event types (L2 snapshots, order-flow, integrity, heartbeat, historical bars, etc.) pass through unconditionally — only data that was produced in real-time from a provider carries the invariants the validators check.

Per-symbol relaxation is opt-in: add "UseRelaxedValidation": true to a symbol's entry in appsettings.json to apply the historical (lenient) F# preset — useful for illiquid instruments, preferreds, or wide-spread options where the default 5-minute timestamp window or strict spread check would produce excessive false positives.
Validation outcomes are tracked by and exported to Prometheus via on the periodic update cycle.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.FSharpEventValidator.#ctor(Meridian.Contracts.Configuration.SymbolConfig[],System.Boolean,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{Meridian.Application.Pipeline.FSharpEventValidator})
member.summary: Initialises the validator.
member.param: Optional array of configured symbols. Symbols with UseRelaxedValidation = true are validated with the lenient historical preset instead of the default preset.
When , the strict real-time preset is applied for non-relaxed symbols (5-second max timestamp age). When (default), the standard default preset is used (5-minute max timestamp age).

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.HotPathBatchSerializer
member.summary: Batch-serializes hot-path struct events (, ) to JSONL-formatted UTF-8 bytes.

remarks.para: A pre-allocated is reused across calls so that the backing byte array grows at most logarithmically and is never released to the GC between batches. A is reset (not re-constructed) before each batch to avoid repeated small allocations in the consumer loop.
Not thread-safe. A single instance must only be used from the consumer thread that drains the corresponding .

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.HotPathBatchSerializer.#ctor(Meridian.Core.Performance.SymbolTable,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a new serializer backed by the given symbol table.
member.param: Shared symbol table used to resolve integer symbol IDs back to strings. Must be the same instance used by the producing ring-buffer writer.
Initial byte capacity of the pre-allocated write buffer. The buffer grows automatically when the batch exceeds this size. Default is 64 KiB — sufficient for ~500 trade events.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.HotPathBatchSerializer.SerializeTrades(System.ReadOnlySpan{Meridian.Core.Performance.RawTradeEvent})
member.summary: Serializes a batch of structs to JSONL UTF-8 bytes.
member.remarks: The returned is valid only until the next call to or on this instance (the underlying buffer is reused). Copy the bytes if longer lifetime is needed.
member.param: Span of trade events to serialize.
member.returns: UTF-8 encoded JSONL bytes — one JSON object per line, terminated by \n. Returns an empty span when is empty.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.HotPathBatchSerializer.SerializeQuotes(System.ReadOnlySpan{Meridian.Core.Performance.RawQuoteEvent})
member.summary: Serializes a batch of structs to JSONL UTF-8 bytes.
member.remarks: The returned is valid only until the next call to or on this instance.
member.param: Span of quote events to serialize.
member.returns: UTF-8 encoded JSONL bytes — one JSON object per line, terminated by \n. Returns an empty span when is empty.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.SecurityMaster.NullSecurityMasterQueryService
member.summary: No-op implementations of all Security Master service interfaces, registered when MERIDIAN_SECURITY_MASTER_CONNECTION_STRING is not configured. Query operations return null / empty collections so that the Minimal API endpoint handlers can return 404 / empty JSON as appropriate. Command and write operations throw so that the caller receives an HTTP 500 that surfaces the configuration requirement. These stubs ensure that ASP.NET Core Minimal API routing initializes correctly even without a Security Master database, preventing startup failures in unconfigured or test environments.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.SecurityMaster.NullTradingParametersBackfillService
member.summary: No-op backfill service registered when either Security Master or Polygon credentials are unavailable.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.SecurityMaster.SecurityMasterAggregateRebuilder.GetCorporateActionsAsync(System.Guid,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns all corporate action events for a security in ascending ex-date order, folding the separate CorpActEvent stream into the aggregate view for a security.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.SecurityMaster.SecurityMasterCanonicalSymbolSeedService
member.summary: Seeds the from the Security Master projection cache on startup, and maintains a reverse lookup from canonical ticker to security ID for per-event enrichment in the canonicalization pipeline.

remarks.para: This service is called by after the projection cache has been populated. It is safe to call multiple times (idempotent: the last call wins).
The method is thread-safe and suitable for use in hot-path event processing. It reads a volatile snapshot reference so it sees the most recent seed without any per-call locking.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.SecurityMaster.SecurityMasterCanonicalSymbolSeedService.TryGetSecurityId(System.String)
member.summary: Resolves a canonical ticker to its Security Master ID. Returns when the ticker is unknown or the seed has not yet run.

member.summary: Reads all active securities from the projection cache, registers provider-aware symbol definitions into the canonical registry, and atomically replaces the ticker→SecurityId reverse-lookup map.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Services.ConfigurationService
member.summary: Unified configuration service that consolidates all configuration-related operations. This is the single entry point for wizard, auto-config, validation, provider detection, credential resolution, self-healing fixes, and hot reload.

remarks.para: Consolidation Goal: All CLI paths and UI workflows should route through this service to eliminate duplicate validation and environment detection logic.

item.description: Credential resolution from environment variables and config
Configuration validation with detailed error reporting
Self-healing fixes for common configuration issues
Unified pipeline producing ValidatedConfig from any entry point

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Services.ServiceRegistry
member.summary: Centralized registry for application services providing discovery, categorization, and dependency resolution capabilities.
member.remarks:
The service registry provides: - Service discovery by category - Service metadata for documentation - Centralized access to common services - Health check aggregation for services
Services are organized into logical categories: - Setup: ConfigurationWizard, AutoConfigurationService, ConfigEnvironmentOverride - Validation: CredentialValidationService, ConnectivityTestService, PreflightChecker - Diagnostics: DiagnosticBundleService, ErrorTracker, FriendlyErrorFormatter - Lifecycle: GracefulShutdownService, StartupSummary - Data: HistoricalDataQueryService, DryRunService, SampleDataGenerator - Calendar: TradingCalendar - Reporting: ProgressDisplayService, DailySummaryWebhook, ApiDocumentationService

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Subscriptions.Services.AutoResubscribePolicy
member.summary:
Auto-resubscribe policy that triggers resubscription when integrity events occur. Implements rate limiting and circuit breaker pattern to prevent cascading failures.
Flow: 1. Integrity event received → check rate limit → check circuit breaker → resubscribe 2. If resubscription fails → increment failure count → possibly open circuit 3. Circuit opens after threshold failures → waits break duration → half-open → test

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Subscriptions.Services.AutoResubscribePolicy.CurrentCircuitState
member.summary: Current state of the global circuit breaker.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Subscriptions.Services.AutoResubscribePolicy.SymbolsInCooldown
member.summary: Number of symbols currently in cooldown.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Subscriptions.Services.AutoResubscribePolicy.SymbolsWithOpenCircuit
member.summary: Number of symbols with open circuit breakers.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Subscriptions.Services.AutoResubscribePolicy.OnIntegrityEventAsync(System.String,Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Enums.IntegritySeverity,Meridian.Application.Config.AppConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Handles an integrity event and potentially triggers resubscription. This is the main entry point for the policy.
member.param: The symbol that experienced the integrity event.
member.returns: True if resubscription was triggered, false if skipped.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Subscriptions.Services.AutoResubscribePolicy.OnDepthIntegrityEventAsync(Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.DepthIntegrityEvent,Meridian.Application.Config.AppConfig,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Handles a depth integrity event.

member.summary: Handles a trade integrity event.

member.summary: Manually resets the circuit breaker to closed state. Use with caution.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Subscriptions.Services.AutoResubscribePolicy.ResetSymbolCircuit(System.String)
member.summary: Resets the circuit breaker for a specific symbol.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Subscriptions.Services.AutoResubscribePolicy.SymbolResubscribeState
member.summary: Per-symbol resubscription state tracking.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Subscriptions.Services.AutoResubscribeOptions
member.summary: Configuration options for the auto-resubscribe policy.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Subscriptions.Services.AutoResubscribeOptions.MinSeverityForResubscribe
member.summary: Minimum severity level required to trigger auto-resubscribe. Default: Error (only Error-level integrity events trigger resubscription).

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Subscriptions.Services.AutoResubscribeOptions.SymbolCooldown
member.summary: Cooldown period after a successful resubscription. Prevents rapid repeated resubscriptions for the same symbol. Default: 30 seconds.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Subscriptions.Services.AutoResubscribeOptions.MinResubscribeInterval
member.summary: Minimum interval between resubscription attempts for the same symbol. Applies even if previous attempt failed. Default: 5 seconds.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Subscriptions.Services.AutoResubscribeOptions.CircuitBreakerThreshold
member.summary: Number of consecutive failures before the global circuit breaker opens. Default: 5 failures.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Subscriptions.Services.AutoResubscribeOptions.CircuitBreakerDuration
member.summary: Duration the global circuit breaker remains open before transitioning to half-open. Default: 60 seconds.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Subscriptions.Services.AutoResubscribeOptions.SymbolCircuitBreakerThreshold
member.summary: Number of consecutive failures before a per-symbol circuit breaker opens. Default: 3 failures.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Subscriptions.Services.AutoResubscribeOptions.SymbolCircuitBreakerDuration
member.summary: Duration a per-symbol circuit breaker remains open. Default: 120 seconds (longer than global to allow global recovery first).

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Subscriptions.SubscriptionOrchestrator
member.summary:
Applies AppConfig symbol changes at runtime (hot reload). Responsible for: - registering symbols with collectors (domain) - subscribing/unsubscribing market depth (infrastructure) via IMarketDataClient - tracking option contract subscriptions for equity and index options
Trades are currently always accepted by TradeDataCollector, but this class is future-proofed to support explicit per-symbol trade subscriptions once you wire them in (tick-by-tick reqs).

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Subscriptions.SubscriptionOrchestrator.ActiveSubscriptionCount
member.summary: Gets the total number of active subscriptions (trades + depth + options).

member.summary: Returns true if the given symbol config represents an option contract.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Wizard.Metadata.ProviderDescriptor
member.summary: Immutable descriptor for a single data provider. Centralises signup URLs, environment variable names, capabilities and priority so that both ConfigurationWizard and AutoConfigurationService can share the same source of truth.
member.param: Human-readable name shown in the wizard UI.
Env-vars that must all be set for the provider to be considered "configured". Empty means no credentials required (e.g. Yahoo, Stooq).
Alternative env-var names accepted in place of .
Provider capability tags: RealTime, Historical, Trades, Quotes, L2Depth, Aggregates, Daily, etc.
Lower value = higher preference during auto-configuration ordering.
Where users sign up for a free or paid tier.
Short description of the free plan limits.
